pattern: Sort the cut positions, compute the maximum gap between consecutive cuts (including edges), then multiply the largest horizontal gap by the largest vertical gap modulo 10^9+7.
hints: After sorting, the height of each horizontal slice is the difference between consecutive cut positions (with 0 and h as implicit boundaries); similarly for vertical.
The maximum piece area is the product of the widest horizontal gap and the widest vertical gap.
With h and w up to 10^9, intermediate products can overflow a 32-bit integer — use 64-bit arithmetic and take the modulo only at the final multiplication.

edge_inputs.description: Single horizontal and vertical cut each, equal spacing
Cut at position 1 — narrow strip at top
Large h and w — overflow trap if using 32-bit
Many cuts that are dense in the middle — max gap is near an edge

stress.description: Random cuts in a large cake
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    h = 10**9
    w = 10**9
    hc = rng.sample(range(1, h), min(size, h - 1))
    vc = rng.sample(range(1, w), min(size, w - 1))
    return [h, w, hc, vc]

complexity.time: O(m log m + n log n)
